scenario_title: Duplicate add_rr for the same RRset members
test_rationale: This test isolates the underspecified intra-message duplicate-add case by sending one standards-compliant UPDATE with two identical add_rr records for the same A RR. Because the zone initially already contains the RR, the server must decide whether to treat the second add as a harmless duplicate, merge it away, or fail the UPDATE. A follow-up QUERY on the touched name records the post-state so Ferret can distinguish acceptance with suppression from outright rejection.

group_1.behavior: Accepts the duplicate add_rrs, suppresses the duplicate as meaningless, and stores a single A RR.
group_1.post_state: host1.campus.edu. has one A RR (rrset present once).

group_2.behavior: Treats the duplicated add_rr as an UPDATE error and rejects the message, leaving the zone unchanged.
group_2.post_state: host1.campus.edu. remains with the original single A RR from the initial zone file.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3 and §3.4 define UPDATE message structure and processing but do not say how a server must handle two identical add_rr operations for the same owner/type/RDATA within one packet. RFC 2181 §5 says duplicates are meaningless and servers should suppress such duplicates if encountered, but it does not clearly bind UPDATE semantics or mandate a specific RCODE. Therefore implementations may either coalesce/suppress the duplicate and return NOERROR, or reject the packet with an error such as SERVFAIL while leaving state unchanged.
